title: VPN Kill Switch: What It Is and Why You Need It
description: A VPN kill switch cuts your internet if the VPN drops, preventing IP exposure. Here is how it works, why it matters, and which VPNs have reliable kill switches.
content:
## What a Kill Switch Does

A kill switch monitors your VPN connection and cuts all internet traffic immediately if the VPN disconnects. Without one, a dropped VPN connection reverts to your regular ISP connection, exposing your real IP address. This happens silently, without any notification, which means you may not realize the exposure occurred.

## When Kill Switches Matter Most

For most everyday VPN use (accessing streaming services, using public WiFi), a brief IP exposure during a connection drop is a minor inconvenience. For activities where IP exposure has real consequences (torrenting on a copyright-sensitive network, journalism in a restrictive country, whistleblowing), a kill switch is not optional.

## System-Level vs App-Level Kill Switches

VPN apps implement kill switches in two ways. An app-level kill switch monitors the VPN app process and blocks traffic when it detects a disconnection. A system-level kill switch uses firewall rules at the OS level to block all non-VPN traffic regardless of what the app does. System-level kill switches are more reliable because they work even if the VPN app crashes completely.

## Which VPNs Have Reliable Kill Switches

Mullvad uses a system-level firewall kill switch by default. It is the most reliable implementation available. ProtonVPN has a system-level kill switch that works on Windows, Mac, and Linux. NordVPN and ExpressVPN have kill switches but they are app-level on some platforms. Test before you trust: connect to your VPN, start downloading a file, then manually kill the VPN process. If the download continues, the kill switch is not working.

## Testing Your Kill Switch

Connect your VPN. Open a browser and go to an IP-check site. Note your masked IP. Open Task Manager (Windows) or Activity Monitor (Mac). Force-kill the VPN process. Immediately check the IP-check site again. If your real IP appears, the kill switch did not work.

## Recommendation

If you need a kill switch that actually works, use Mullvad or ProtonVPN with the kill switch enabled and system-level firewall option selected. Test it once after setup. Do not assume it works without testing.
